segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Wind met them like a blade honed on snow.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The world was all angles -- black rock, white glare, a sky so close it felt like a lid pressing down on the peaks.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Breath came thin and loud in their own ears, each inhalation a small victory against the altitude.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ridge ran like a spine, dropping to glazed couloirs on either side -- sheets of ice that fell away into clouds and nothing. One wrong step and gravity would have opinions.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Far ahead, the flame-path in Caelin's vision brightened to a fine wire, then split along the crest -- two threads, left and lower, right and steep.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He blinked; the right-hand thread tugged harder. Not safer. Just certain. The kind of certainty that didn't care about safety.

segments.speaker: elowen
segments.text: "Two ways,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Then there's only one,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex answered, already squinting into the glare, reading the terrain with the practiced eye of someone who'd survived by always choosing the less-bad option.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Sunlight cut through high cloud and made three mock-suns at the horizon -- halos stacked like coins, a parhelion that shouldn't have been so clear at this time of day.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For a moment the middle ring threw nine spokes across the glare, a trick of ice crystals and angle.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They watched until the spokes thinned and died, melting back into ordinary light.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Pretty,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik said, too softly.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "I hate it."

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Atmospheric phenomenon,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex said.

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "Ice crystals refracting -- "

segments.speaker: thornik
segments.text: "I know what it is,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik interrupted.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Doesn't make it less ominous."

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Light for the edge,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana said, lifting a hand as they prepared to traverse the exposed ridge.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nothing came.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: She breathed, steadied, tried again. The wardline stuttered and guttered like breath caught in a throat, flickering silver-gold-silver before dying completely.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Source is... thin,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, voice even by force of will.

segments.speaker: serana
segments.text: "Can't hold it."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara's smile didn't travel to her eyes.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "Altitude does that to certainty."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Enough,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said -- quieter than anger, firmer than plea.

segments.speaker: vex
segments.text: "We move slow. No miracles, just rope."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana nodded once and lowered her hand. That, too, was a kind of prayer -- accepting limitation, working within it.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They roped in -- Thornik's knots neat as script, each one tested twice. Vex testing every anchor with insults and physics, trusting neither completely.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ridge narrowed to two boot-widths and a faith wider than comfort.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan took rear without comment. He walked like a man who had been here in other lifetimes and found the drop repetitive. His shadow stayed small, obedient, costly.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Halfway across, a gust hit sideways -- a wall of wind with malice in it. Elowen's heel skated on rime; Caelin felt the rope jar tight across his chest, fibers groaning.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Lean in,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, not sure who needed it. Maybe himself.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They did. The gust passed. The ridge became a ridge again.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara altered the angle of her shadow with a single, almost lazy wrist-turn -- subtle magic, barely noticeable.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: For one breath the glare softened where their eyes needed it most, giving them a moment of relief from the sun-blindness.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex saw it and didn't comment. She only moved faster, wanting to be off the exposed spine before the mountain changed its mind.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Shelter,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan said, three heartbeats later.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Not warning. A direction.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They followed his chin-tilt to a bite taken out of the lee side -- a snow-scooped alcove backed by stone, protected from the worst of the wind.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They crouched out of the wind, bodies grateful for even this small mercy. Fire was a joke up here where oxygen barely existed; Thornik didn't try. They huddled together for warmth instead.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana sat with her back to rock and closed her eyes like someone setting down a weight too heavy to carry any longer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin heard the whisper before he saw her jaw tighten -- the overlapping threads of two instructions that would never agree, playing in an endless loop.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Protect the flame.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Offer the bearer.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Her fingers found the chalk tucked in her belt and snapped it cleanly in two. The pieces fell into the snow and were immediately buried by a fresh gust.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "I can walk,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she said, eyes still shut.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "I can't promise more."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "That's enough,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin answered.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: He didn't add, For now.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Vex distributed a stingy fistful of dried fruit as if paying out wages.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Don't die; I'll have to redo headcount."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik chewed slowly, trying not to look at the halos still staining the horizon like cosmic warning signs.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Quantitatively speaking, we're under-supplied on miracles."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Nyxara watched the way the wind curled around Caelin's forearm, as if the air had opinions about ownership.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Quantitatively speaking,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: she murmured,

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "we're over-supplied on audience."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Durgan didn't sit. His shadow behaved itself. That felt like a decision that cost him.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The ridge underfoot hummed -- two notes a tone apart, beating slow against each other in a rhythm that made teeth ache.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Thornik touched stone with a bare palm.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "That's not weather."

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen listened with her whole body, the way she'd learned to read the forest's moods.

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Something turned over below us."

segments.speaker: caelin
segments.text: "Left,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin said. The pull in his arm was no longer a suggestion. It was a command.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: No one argued.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They moved into a light that felt older than noon, shadows stretching wrong directions, the sun pale and distant.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: The mock-suns had faded, but the taste of metal hadn't -- copper and ozone, the flavor of lightning about to strike.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Behind them, the wind erased their prints in a single pass, as if the mountain wanted no record they'd been there.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Ahead, the path narrowed and began to sing -- a low, thrumming note that rose from stone or sky or the space between.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Serana opened her hand for light one more time and found none. She closed it before anyone saw, fingers curling into a fist.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: But Caelin saw. And said nothing.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Before they moved, Vex ran her count aloud -- four charges spent, one blade chipped, three wounds that needed proper dressing when there was time. Thornik tapped the scale of remaining fuses against his thigh: six left.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Elowen said nothing, but turned one wrist up; two more vine-marks had darkened at the edges, thin black lines where the green should have been. Serana's wardlight was gone and hadn't come back.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: Caelin looked at his arm. Coal-glow. Steady. Not spent, not surging -- just present.

segments.speaker: durgan
segments.text: "Still here,"

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: he said, to no one in particular.

segments.speaker: narrator
segments.text: They descended.
